summary: Ukraine warns of a possible Iskander-M ballistic missile launch from occupied Crimea. Russian infrastructure in Taganrog, Voronezh and Tuapse suffered air attacks, while Ukrainian forces reported shooting down 150 hostile drones. Civilian casualties in Ukraine are reported at 70‑100 per week due to drone, missile and bomb strikes.
key_points: Threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launch from occupied Crimea (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67240).
Russian facilities hit: Taganrog plant, Voronezh "Baltimor" airfield and Tuapse refinery attacked by Ukrainian strikes (Source: @UkraineNow/81480).
Ukrainian authorities report 70‑100 civilian deaths weekly from drone, ballistic and cruise missile attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81472).
Ukrainian air defence claims 150 hostile Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and imitation drones shot down across north, south and east (Source: @kpszsu/62966).
Belarusian officials allege 116 Ukrainian drone incursions across the Belarus border in one week, which Ukraine denies (Source: @UkraineNow/81476).
Energy infrastructure in Enerhodar suffered a mass UAV attack with over 50 explosions, causing power outages (Source: @tass_agency/377714).
Ukrainian Ministry of Internal Affairs cites ongoing attacks on civilian areas, including explosions heard in Donetsk (Source: @UkraineNow/81480).

sections.executive_summary: Threat of Iskander‑M missile launch from occupied Crimea raises strategic risk. Ukrainian forces have struck Russian industrial targets in Taganrog, Voronezh and Tuapse. Civilian casualties in Ukraine remain high, with 70‑100 deaths weekly from aerial attacks.

points: Ukrainian officials warned that occupied Crimea could be used to launch Iskander‑M ballistic missiles, indicating a potential escalation in long‑range strike capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67240).

The Ministry of Internal Affairs reported that Ukrainian territories suffer 70‑100 civilian deaths each week due to drone, ballistic missile, cruise missile and aerial bomb attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81472).

Ukrainian air defence units claim to have intercepted 150 hostile Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and imitation drones across the country, with eight strike drones hitting seven locations (Source: @kpszsu/62966).

Ukrainian forces conducted air strikes on Russian infrastructure, hitting a plant in Taganrog, the "Baltimor" airfield in Voronezh and the Tuapse refinery for the fifth time this spring (Source: @UkraineNow/81480).

Ukrainian drones were reported operating over multiple northern oblasts, including Kyiv, Chernihiv and Zhytomyr, targeting locations such as Ivanivka, Krasiatichi and Chernobyl (Source: @kpszsu/62955, @kpszsu/62958).
Two civilians were injured in Taganrog during an aerial attack, according to the city mayor (Source: @tass_agency/377706).
A missile struck the building of the Southern Directorate of the Central Bank in Sevastopol (Source: @tass_agency/377712).
Enerhodar’s Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant experienced a massive UAV assault with more than 50 explosions, resulting in power outages but no casualties (Source: @tass_agency/377714).
Russian defence officials reported that 140 Ukrainian drones were shot down over Russian regions during the previous night (Source: @tass_agency/377709).
Belarusian authorities claimed 116 Ukrainian drone incursions across the Belarus border in a week, a claim denied by Ukraine (Source: @UkraineNow/81476).
Ukrainian forces reported explosions heard in Donetsk, suggesting ongoing combat activity on the eastern front (Source: @UkraineNow/81480).
Shahed and other strike drones were reported attacking from Kursk, Bryansk, Oryol, Millerovo and Shatalovo directions, affecting eastern sectors (Source: @kpszsu/62966).
Ukrainian drones were observed over Sumy region heading southwest from Bilopillia, indicating operational activity in the northeast (Source: @kpszsu/62933).
Ukrainian air defence claimed eight strike drones hit seven locations across the east, demonstrating continued aerial contest (Source: @kpszsu/62966).
Multiple UAV sightings reported over northern Kyiv region, including flights toward Ivanivka and Krasiatichi (Source: @kpszsu/62955).
Ukrainian drones were observed over Chernihiv heading from the east and from Bryansk, indicating cross‑border activity (Source: @kpszsu/62958, @eRadarrua/93846).
Ukrainian UAVs were tracked moving from the north‑west of Lviv towards Zhovkva/Lviv, showing activity on the north‑western front (Source: @kpszsu/62968).
A group of UAVs from Bryansk was reported moving along the Belarus border toward Słavutych, highlighting northern pressure points (Source: @kpszsu/62935).
Ukrainian forces claim to have struck the Tuapse oil refinery for the fifth time this spring (Source: @UkraineNow/81480).
Enerhodar in the south experienced a large UAV attack with over 50 explosions, causing power outages (Source: @tass_agency/377714).
Ukrainian drones were reported over the Kherson region, though specific targets were not detailed (Source: @kpszsu/62955).
Ukrainian air defence reported downing hostile drones across the southern sector as part of the 150‑drone interception (Source: @kpszsu/62966).
Ukrainian air defence reported shooting down 150 hostile drones of various types, including Shahed, Gerbera and Italmas, across north, south and east (Source: @kpszsu/62966).
Russian defence ministry announced that 140 Ukrainian drones were downed over Russian territory in the previous night (Source: @tass_agency/377709).
Ukrainian strikes hit Russian air assets, including the "Baltimor" airfield in Voronezh (Source: @UkraineNow/81480).
Enerhodar’s power grid suffered a UAV attack with heavy hexacopter "Baba‑yaga" and FPV drones, indicating advanced UAV capabilities used by Ukraine (Source: @tass_agency/377714).

key_developments: The possibility of an Iskander‑M ballistic missile launch from occupied Crimea has been highlighted, raising concerns of long‑range strike escalation. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67240)

Ukrainian air strikes damaged a plant in Taganrog, the "Baltimor" airfield in Voronezh and the Tuapse refinery, marking significant hits on Russian industrial infrastructure. (Source: @UkraineNow/81480)
Ukrainian authorities report 70‑100 civilian deaths per week from drone, ballistic missile, cruise missile and bomb attacks, indicating a high civilian toll. (Source: @UkraineNow/81472)
Ukrainian air defence claims to have shot down 150 hostile Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and imitation drones across multiple fronts, showing robust anti‑drone operations. (Source: @kpszsu/62966)

Belarusian officials allege 116 Ukrainian drone incursions across the Belarus border in one week, a claim denied by Ukrainian officials, reflecting information‑war tensions. (Source: @UkraineNow/81476)

Enerhodar’s Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant suffered a mass UAV attack with over 50 explosions, causing power outages but no casualties, highlighting vulnerability of critical energy sites. (Source: @tass_agency/377714)
Russian defence ministry reported downing 140 Ukrainian drones over Russian territory in a single night, indicating intensified aerial contest over border regions. (Source: @tass_agency/377709)
sections.threat_assessment: Ukraine faces a multi‑dimensional threat environment, with potential long‑range Iskander‑M launches from Crimea and sustained drone attacks causing civilian casualties. Russian infrastructure remains vulnerable to Ukrainian precision strikes, while Russian air defences are actively intercepting Ukrainian UAVs. The contested airspace over both sides' borders suggests a high risk of escalation and further civilian impact.
sections.regional_response: Western diplomatic missions, including EU embassies, have affirmed they will remain in Kyiv despite Russian warnings, indicating continued political support for Ukraine. Belarusian officials are attempting to portray Ukrainian drone activity as provocations, while Ukraine denies these claims, maintaining its narrative of defensive operations. International observers note the growing use of advanced UAVs and the strategic targeting of energy facilities as key elements of the conflict.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source monitoring shows a surge in UAV activity across northern, eastern and southern fronts, with multiple cross‑border trajectories from Russian and Belarusian territories. The reported 150‑drone interception suggests improved Ukrainian air‑defence coordination. Russian claims of high UAV losses may be inflated for propaganda, but the documented attacks on critical infrastructure underscore the operational significance of UAVs in the current war phase.
